entities.name: TILLAGE-INDUCED MICRORELIEF
entities.description: The physical variation in surface height caused by tillage operations.

entities.name: SURFACE CRUSTS
entities.description: Hardened layers on the soil surface resulting from rainfall and tillage.

entities.name: OXIC PALEUSTULT
entities.description: A specific type of sandy soil found in southern Togo.

entities.name: SOUTHERN TOGO
entities.description: The geographic region where the soil study was conducted.

entities.name: NATURAL RAINFALL
entities.description: The environmental precipitation that triggered crust formation.

entities.name: SURFACE TOPOGRAPHY
entities.description: The physical features and elevation of the land surface.

entities.name: MICROMORPHOLOGICAL ANALYSIS
entities.description: The technique used to examine the internal structure of undisturbed crust samples.

entities.name: RUNOFF CRUSTS
entities.description: Type 1 crusts characterized by superposed clay bands buried in a sand layer.

entities.name: EROSION CRUSTS
entities.description: Type 2 crusts characterized by a thin exposed clay band.

entities.name: CLAY BANDS
entities.description: Layers of clay particles within the soil crust structure.

entities.name: MICROMASS-DEPLETED SAND LAYER
entities.description: A layer of sand lacking fine particles, often containing clay bands.

entities.name: INITIAL MICROTOPOGRAPHY
entities.description: The surface topography measured at the beginning of the study.

entities.name: FINAL MICROTOPOGRAPHY
entities.description: The surface topography measured at the end of the study.

entities.name: EROSION AND DEPOSITING PROCESSES
entities.description: Physical processes that govern the development and distribution of soil crusts.

entities.name: CRUST GENESIS MODEL OF VALENTIN AND BRESSON
entities.description: A theoretical model explaining the formation of soil crusts.
